programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.sub-title: Home Sweet Home: Meals in Minutes
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is making quick family meals at home on the ranch. Theres Pork Marsala with Mushrooms and a gorgeous Grilled Shrimp Panzanella. Plus, Ree makes Spinach Artichoke Calzones and shortcut Creamy Tortellini Soup, a real 10-minute wonder.

programme.title: Jamies Easy Meals for Every Day
programme.sub-title: Crispy Chicken and Sweet Potato Burger
programme.desc: Jamie rustles up a quick crispy breaded chicken for the family and he shows how to transform a burger by using sweet potato in two new ways.

programme.title: Jamies Easy Meals for Every Day
programme.desc: Jamie cooks up a baked casserole and he shows a way to dress an aubergine salad, served with the quickest homemade flatbreads.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Inas taking an Italian trip, and cooking up classics and new takes on her favourite Italian dishes. This includes delicious tomatoes roasted with pesto and parmesan.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina makes some teatime treats. There are red velvet cupcakes, shortbread cookies and orange curd, plus afternoon-tea inspired flower arrangements from Inas florist friend Beth.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The bakers bread making skills are tested with three increasingly complicated challenges over two days. They start with the signature bake, a free-form flavoured loaf that produces a variety of interesting results. The technical challenge is focaccia, which really separates the wheat from the chaff. Finally, a mammoth six-hour hour challenge requires the bakers to create a display bread basket.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The bakers take on biscuits and these bite-sized, delicate delights prove too much for some. The remaining eight must impress legendary cookery writer Mary Berry and artisan baker Paul Hollywood with their interpretation of a classic biscuit. Who will crumble when it comes to judging and whose ginger nuts are too hot to handle?

programme.title: James Martins United Cakes of America
programme.desc: James tries a Pimlico Cake in Baltimore, before heading for the Maryland countryside to taste a cake which is only made for six weeks of the year.

programme.title: James Martins United Cakes of America
programme.desc: In Lancaster, Pennsylvania, James eats shoofly pie, before trying his hand at twisting pretzels.

programme.title: James Martins United Cakes of America
programme.desc: The cake-loving chef heads into Philadelphia, makes a Fire and Ice cake, and hunts for the notorious pumpple cake, which is baked with sweet pies inside.

programme.title: James Martins United Cakes of America
programme.desc: Foodie fun as James samples Butter Cake and meets a man who has brought a taste of Florida to Brooklyn, making authentic Key Lime Pies.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Mississippi Adventure
programme.desc: The Hairy Bikers continue their Mississippi adventures. In New Orleans, they meet a living blues legend and get to grips with some of the best local food.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.sub-title: Home Sweet Home: Labor Day Spread
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is serving up easy, delicious Labor Day eats. There are Crispy Wings with Hot Honey Sauce and spicy Pimento Cheese Grits. For something sweet, Ree makes divine Peach Shortcakes and a refreshing Ginger Cherry Limeade Spritzer.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by top TV chef Monica Galetti. Theyre in Oxford, where Monica discovers the delights of an organic local distiller. James takes a river trip to a fantastic pick your own farm where he picks some raspberries and he uses them to cook a delicious pudding on the banks of the river. James also visits one of the worlds best chefs, Raymond Blanc, at his restaurant Le Manoir aux QuatSaisons, where he enjoys some of Raymonds Michelin-starred dishes.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is in South Wales, accompanied by Michelin-starred chef Stephen Terry. James discovers award-winning Welsh wine and cooks a Welsh lamb ragu on a beautiful mountainside. He samples the delights of organic Welsh chocolate and visits cooking legend Shaun Hill at his renowned restaurant The Walnut Tree. The day comes to an end at Barry Island, where James and Stephen enjoy all the fairground rides at this famous resort and James cooks Barry Island churros overlooking the beach.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy hits up three American cities for big international flavours. He visits a Filipino joint in LA, samples Ethiopian cuisine in San Francisco and bites into Indian classics in Minneapolis.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy visits a multi-generational joint bringing NOLA to LA, a unique Minneapolis deli serving up meatless `meats and a Hawaiian noodle spot dishing out ramen and Vietnamese crepes.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: From wild boar gnocchi in Los Angeles to a lamb belly BLT in Miami, Guys grabbing grub cooked up in pubs across the US.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy hits up Minneapolis for a family pizza-making tradition, Miami for Haitian classics and Portsmouth, New Hampshire, for global-inspired street food.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman chomps his way across America. He takes on the Jumbolie Challenge, attempting to eat a five pound Stromboli in less than an hour.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman travels across the United States to find the countrys best traditional food, while undertaking tough eating challenges along the way.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.sub-title: Helmet Cake, Healthy Mauro, and Huge Trainers
programme.desc: It is National Train Day and Carlos team makes a cake complete with tunnels for model trains. Joey wants to make a firefighter-themed cake and Mauros son helps him get healthy.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Buddy makes a cake for Haagen-Dazs 50th anniversary while also making a cake for the Broadway show Come Fly Away. However orders prove difficult to fill when the ovens stop working.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Mississippi Adventure

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.sub-title: Home Sweet Home: Easy Weeknight Dinners
programme.desc: Ree Drummond shares her Cajun Surf and Turf Pasta, Pineapple Chicken Stir-Fry. Shortcut Broccoli and Tomato Flatbread Pizzas; Street Corn Quesadilla. These speedy suppers are ideal for Monday to Friday meals that need to be ready in moments.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond shares her Italian Pork Sandwiches and crowd-pleasing Butternut Squash and Black Bean Enchiladas; Slow-Cooker Teriyaki Ribs and a Greek 7-Layer Dip thats perfect for fall.

programme.title: Jamies Easy Meals for Every Day
programme.sub-title: Farmhouse Roast Chicken and Creamy Prawn Linguine
programme.desc: Jamie gives traditional roast chicken a delicious French twist; shows us tasty ways to use up stale bread; and cooks his take on a creamy prawn linguine for his friend and mentor Gennaro Contaldo.

programme.title: Jamies Easy Meals for Every Day
programme.sub-title: Roast Chicken Margherita and Prawn Toast Toastie

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Honey white bread; open tuna and hummus sandwich; mussels with basil.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: In round five, everyone is out to avoid the dreaded soggy bottom as the seven remaining bakers face pies. Theyll begin with their signature family pie, before the dreaded technical challenge which sees lifelong vegetarian Jason delivering six beautifully baked and seasoned pork pies to the judging table. Finally, there is a sweet showstopper challenge in the shape of a meringue pie.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its an all-lady quarter-final as the remaining five have to impress with their dessert skills. In the signature bake, judges Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood are expecting to see and taste baked cheesecakes that reach their high expectations. The technical challenge is the bete noir of most bakers - a chocolate roulade. Finally, hundreds of choux pastry profiteroles must be perfected.

programme.title: James Martins United Cakes of America
programme.desc: James visits a bakery made famous by `Sex and the City. Back in the kitchen, James whips up a strawberry gateaux and has some fun with cornflake cookies.

programme.desc: Jamess cake adventures leads him to find out about two of New Yorks classic puddings, the New York cheesecake and Baked Alaska.

programme.title: James Martins United Cakes of America

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy tries all cosmopolitan comfort food. He samples corned beef and cabbage in Portsmouth, New Hampshire, authentic Ethiopian specialties in Los Angeles and over-the-top hot dogs in Minneapolis.

programme.desc: Guys picking up burgers to buns to bagels. In Portsmouth, NH, he visits a deli serving lamb burgers and ribs, and in Los Angeles, a Koreatown favourite.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy stops in small towns for big-time eats. In Raymond, NH, a butchery cranks out corn dogs from scratch. In Rocky Hill, NJ, a 1700s inn-turned-gastropub dishes out a tomato twist on burgers and disco fries.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guys going coast-to-coast discovering righteous flavours in Oregon and New Hampshire. Check out an octopus dish and classic biscuits and gravy in Portland, Oregon, then head to Portsmouth, New Hampshire, for pork belly tacos.

programme.desc: In Des Moines, Adam takes on the Adam Emmenecker Challenge at Jethros BBQ: 15 minutes to finish a mammoth sandwich.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The atmosphere in the kitchen gets even more heated and the veteran chef is expecting perfection from the fish course. Ally McGrath creates an inventive dish based on his summer memories of rock pooling in the River Tweed.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Angela Malik is in her element, cooking a tiffin box based on memories of summer picnics. Michael Bremner is taking a huge risk, and Ally is creating an inventive dish based on a summer barbeque.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. Angela Malik is cooking a technically challenging dessert.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two Scottish chefs must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver. They are joined by guest judge Gary Parsons.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Determined newcomers Paul Croasdale and Nick Brodie take on returning chef Phil Carmichael for a place in the national finals. Its Phils third time in the competition and this year hes determined to make it to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The pressure in the kitchen is high as the chefs prepare their fish courses. Paul Croasdale is trying to recreate the elegance of Wimbledon with luxury ingredients such as king crab and scallops.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the main course and all three chefs are cooking Welsh lamb in hope of impressing. Phil is taking a risk with barbecued lamb and in an attempt to elevate a humble barbecue to banquet level, hes serving it with many accompaniments.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges so all three chefs are feeling the pressure. Phil Carmichael creates a tennis ball dessert that combines two British classics, Eton mess and trifle.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two winning chefs from the Wales region must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges and guest judge Dan Bloxham, Wimbledons Master of Ceremonies.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With two spots left in the National Finals, its the turn of the Central chefs to battle it out. Pip Lacey, head chef at the Michelin starred Murano in London, tried her luck in the competition two years ago. Shes takes on two newcomers.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With the chefs hoping to impress, they each take risks. Pip Lacey combines lobster with strawberry in a tribute to strawberries and cream. Ryan Simpsons also using lobster in his recreation of a BBQ and has invented a technique to create his chips.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Today the chefs cook their main courses and have very different takes on the Taste of Summer brief. Ryan Simpson hopes to elevate a ploughmans lunch into a dish worthy of the banquet, and Nick Deverell-Smith celebrates a meat he thinks is underused.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges. Pip Laceys pineapple dessert uses coriander. Nick Deverell-Smith also pays tribute to the pineapple, creating a dessert featuring a rice pudding made with cous-cous.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs whove made it through cook their four courses again. They must impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver and guest judge Jordanne Whiley, one of Britains most successful tennis players.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs meet their surprise veteran judge and cook their starters. Eddie is hoping to do better than last year, Joery is hoping to impress with a tribute to Richard Krajicek, and Tommy is creating a warm salad.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Joery Castel is looking to triumph by combing mackerel and cucumber. Tommy Heaney hopes his Murray Mound is a fitting tribute to Andi Murray, Eddie Attwell is creating five different strawberry elements to accompany the scallops in his dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tommy Heaneys BBQ dish is a surf and turf combination, Joery Castels aiming to recreate a more American style BBQ and Eddie Attwells taking a risk with an unusual meat.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tommy Heaneys BBQ dish is a surf and turf combination, Joery Castels aiming to recreate a more American style BBQ and Eddie Attwells taking a risk with an unusual meat.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs whove made it through cook their four courses again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver who are joined this week by tennis greatest entertainer, Mansour Bahrami.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With Finals Week ahead of them, judge Andi Oliver first summons the chefs to Wimbledon to take a look at where they could be cooking if they win. The judges are joined by guest judge Sue Barker, host of the Wimbledon coverage for more than 20 years.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs must cook their fish courses but with some exceptionally high scoring dishes in the regional heats, each chef will need to deliver perfection in order to see off the competition. The judges are joined by guest judge Tim Henman.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is main course day and with only two spots remaining on the banquet menu, the competition is fierce. As the barbecues are fired up, all 8 chefs are feeling the heat. The judges are joined by guest judgeand Paralympic gold medallist, Gordon Reid.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is dessert day and with just one place remaining at the Wimbledon banquet, each chef is giving everything they have got. The judges are joined by guest judge Annabel Croft, former British Number One and Junior Wimbledon Champion.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the grand finale and the winning chefs must now cook their dishes at the banquet. Once in the kitchen, the chefs face the huge challenge of scaling up their dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Competing for top spot are Danny Parker, head chef at Michelin starred `House of Tides in Newcastle, who is back to try his luck in the competition again after being knocked out in the regional heat last year following a tense tie-break. Hes taking on two newcomers: Dan Fletcher, protege of Phil Howard, and now head chef at `Fenchurch in the City of London, and David Coulson,who runs `Peace and Loaf in Newcastle, which boasts 2 AA rosettes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After a tense starter course the chefs are all aiming for 10s with their unique fish dishes. David is celebrating a personal connection to the brief with his `My Cod I Bet Me Maam Has Seen Some Trotters, a tribute to his mum who has worked as a chiropodist. Dans `For All Those Sleepless Nights pays tribute to nightshift workers from the NHS with some bold but classic flavours while Dannys `Not All Superheroes Wear Capes aims to give a taste of luxury to the hardworking staff.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dan pays tribute to an icon of modern nursing, Florence Nightingale, while Danny in inspired by the founder of the NHS, Aneurin Bevan. Davids dish is dedicated to the air ambulance staff who saved the life of his fiance, and he even takes a trip to meet one of them.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the chefs last chance to impress and tensions are high. Dan takes a risk with an unusual combination of ewes milk and bergamot while Danny is determined to pull out all the stops with his complex dessert `Aunty Gwens Chocolate. David hopes to transform a childhood comfort classic into a show stopping dessert. Dan also takes a trip to meet Dr Ben Clarke who was working in the Royal London Hospital AandE during the London Bridge terror attack.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook their four course menus again, but will they take on board their veterans advice as they serve their `Feast To Say Thank You?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Banquet champion Selin Kiazim, a rising star of the London scene renowned for her Turkish Cypriot fusion flavours, is back hoping to join the elite group of chefs who have made it to the banquet twice. She is up against James Cochran whos worked at some of Londons toughest kitchens including 2 Michelin starred `The Ledbury and Scott Goss from `The Twenty Six in Tunbridge Wells, renowned for his use of local Kent ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs do battle for the fish course and the atmosphere in the kitchen is tense. Lobster is catch of the day for James whose dish is Caribbean-inspired. Scott is attempting to elevate a classic fish pie, dedicated to his mum who worked as a pharmacist in the NHS for 23 years. And Selin is taking a risk with a complex three-part fish dish featuring sardines, scallops and langoustines, as a nod to the purported health benefits of a Mediterranean diet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scott is showcasing rare breed Tamworth pork to say thank you to the rare breed of people who work for the NHS. Selin is serving a complex duck dish using ingredients from all around the world to honour `Our Global NHS. And James is presenting five cuts of goat cooked in five different ways, for his bold dish `Under The Knife.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Selin puts her own unique twist on tea and toast. James creates a decadent coconut parfait accompanied by mango puree filled doughnuts for his dessert `Tree of Life inspired by the coconut palm tree, the NHS and both of their contributions to our health. Scott hopes his elevated gypsy tart `Honey Bee Good will secure him a place in the final.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London and the South East chefs must cook their four-course menus again, but will they take on board their veterans advice as they re-make their `Feast To Say Thank You?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Vying to be crowned champion of Scotland are three talented newcomers to the GBM kitchen. Lorna McNee is a former Scottish Chef of the Year and is renowned for her technical precision which she has gained from working her entire career at 2 Michelin-starred restaurant Andrew Fairlie at Gleneagles.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and the chefs are all out to impress. The heat is increased as all three serve their unique takes on lobster. Ross is hoping to snatch victory with his dish `Lobster Aid. Lornas `Live Well, Live Very Well features only three elements, each of which she needs to deliver perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Ross is hoping to impress with `Oh Deer Its Time For Your Penicillin, his tribute to Scottish scientist Alexander Fleming featuring an unusual yeast element as well as venison. Across the kitchen, Lorna, a former Game Chef of the Year, is also cooking venison in a bid to impress.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and the last chance for the chefs to impress before one is sent home. Ross is attempting to elevate a comfort classic into a dish worthy of the banquet. Lornas cooking a personal tribute to the NHS staff who have cared for her during treatment for a rare blood condition, using unusual yuzu and tonka bean flavours. Once again Ben is serving an experimental dish with his `Dance for the Greater Good which includes a bees wax ice cream.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its judging day and the two remaining chefs must impress the panel of Oliver Peyton, Andi Oliver and Matthew Fort. Both chefs are gunning for success and are doing everything they can to deliver their menus to perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its judging day and the two remaining chefs must impress the panel of Oliver Peyton, Andi Oliver and Matthew Fort. Both chefs are gunning for success and are doing everything they can to deliver their menus to perfection.

programme.desc: An in-depth look at the incredible story of the air raid planned by Lt Col Doolittle on the Japanese mainland in retaliation for Pearl Harbor.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Find out how a lobster led archaeologists to the discovery of an 8,000-year-old Neolithic underwater settlement. Join a team at a secret location in Dorset as they excavate an Iron Age crouched burial and in Londons East End,location reporter Naoise Mac Sweeney visits a modern construction site as it gives up the secrets of an Elizabethan playhouse.

programme.title: Cursed Treasures
programme.desc: Archaeologists and scientists help to uncover the truth behind the deadly curses bestowed on the finders of some of the worlds most fascinating treasures.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: The River Hunters head to the River Lochy in search for evidence of the clashing Scottish clans embroiled in the 17th century civil war.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: Rick Edwards joins YouTuber and detectorist Beau Ouimette on an underwater hunt to discover the secrets of Hadrians Wall.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: Rick and Beau discover a variety of intriguing items as they search the rough waters of the River Tees for lost Tudor relics.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones visits Lancaster Castle to explore its grisly history of crime and punishment, including the infamous Lancashire Witches trials and the martyrdom of the Jesuit priest Saint Edmund Arrowsmith.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones visits the heart of the English countryside to explore Arundel Castle and the dramatic history of the Earls of Arundel.

programme.title: Empire Builders
programme.sub-title: Ancient Greece - Minoans, Mycenaeans, and Greek City States
programme.desc: Explore the great historic sites of the Minoan civilisation on Crete, Mycenae in the Peloponnese, and the emerging Greek city states such as Corinth.

programme.title: Empire Builders
programme.desc: Venture back to Ancient Greece and explore the great sites of Athens, Delphi, Olympia and Epidaurus which form some of the greatest sites in antiquity.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: The team are on the hunt for archaeology from the more recent past as they follow the search for artefacts from World War II. They join marine archaeologists in the Solent as they raise the once-in-a-lifetime find of a Fairey Barracuda divebomber. More than 2,500 Barracudas were in service during the war but not a single complete plane survives today. Naoíse Mac Sweeney joins the post excavation to reveal how the find could help bring this rare aircraft back to life.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts follows an entire year of British archaeology, joining up the results of digs and investigations the length of the country. The results are astonishing, and sometimes disturbing. Roman finds include the mystery of 97 babies murdered by the Thames, a fabulous Roman coin hoard found in Somerset and a man buried on a layer of dead animals.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: Rick and Beau discover a variety of intriguing items as they search the rough waters of the River Tees for lost Tudor relics.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: Rick and river-searching expert Beau explore important landmarks on the shores of the River Wear.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice tells the story of Norman England by studying the history of Winchester, Britains most Norman city. Alice attempts to storm a castle in chain mail, investigates the origins of modern surgery and tucks in to an appetising eel pie.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Norwich, the UKs most Tudor city, Professor Alice Roberts learns about religious intolerance, uncovers the Norwich FC canarys origins and experiences humiliating Tudor justice.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tonys walk takes him back to 1940 when the sleepy county of Dorset became the frontline in the war against Hitler. His five-day, 60-mile walk along the Jurassic coast reveals the countys hidden World War II story. Starting by the defences on Chesil Beach, Tonys journey encompasses stunning scenery and amazing acts of ingenuity and bravery as he heads east to finish in Swanage and Studland Bay.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson goes for a walk through some of Britains beautiful and historic landscapes. Tony kicks off the series with a 40-mile walk through the glorious Peak District, along the Derwent Valley, where the worlds industrial revolution was born. This is a journey that reveals how Britain transformed itself from a nation of farmers into the industrial powerhouse of the world.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: A look back at the best archaeological findings of 2014, including the Barrow Clump, Ipplepen and Winchester sites.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Archaeologists search Orkney and the North of Britain, where they discover a Roman altar and a 5,000-year-old temple.

programme.title: Empire Builders
programme.desc: Explore how the 80 Years War left the Spanish Empire financially ruined, while the Dutch Republic, by contrast, became a global commercial power.

programme.title: Empire Builders
programme.desc: After the Dutch Republic declared its independence from Spain, it was cut off from the lucrative spice trades.

programme.title: The Lost Pirate Kingdom
programme.desc: Benjamin Hornigolds crew deposes him for his refusal to attack English ships, but Edward Thatch remains loyal. Later, pirates launch raids on British slavers.

programme.title: The Lost Pirate Kingdom
programme.desc: English authorities tap Woodes Rogers to put a stop to pirate attacks. Black Sam Bellamy suffers a cruel fate at the height of his success.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson heads off for a 45-mile walk across Wiltshire to tell the story of life and death in the last centuries of the Stone Age. His route takes him through the greatest concentration of prehistoric sites in Europe.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: It was 30 years after the Romans invaded Britain that they were ready to take on the challenge of conquering the Lake District. Tony Robinson tracks their 50-mile walk from Penrith past Ullswater to Ambleside and on to the Irish Sea at Ravenglass.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: The team are on the hunt for archaeology from the more recent past as they follow the search for artefacts from World War II. They join marine archaeologists in the Solent as they raise the once-in-a-lifetime find of a Fairey Barracuda divebomber. More than 2,500 Barracudas were in service during the war but not a single complete plane survives today. Naoíse Mac Sweeney joins the post excavation to reveal how the find could help bring this rare aircraft back to life.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts follows an entire year of British archaeology, joining up the results of digs and investigations the length of the country. The results are astonishing, and sometimes disturbing. Roman finds include the mystery of 97 babies murdered by the Thames, a fabulous Roman coin hoard found in Somerset and a man buried on a layer of dead animals.

programme.desc: A 1930s bungalow in Dereham in Norfolk, a first-floor flat in Londons East Ham and a property in Maryport in Cumbria with a hidden room are all sold at auction. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear their plans for their investments.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion visit a semi in Ilkeston in Derby, a flat in Streatham Hill in London and a bungalow in Somersets Portishead.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion visit a fifth-floor flat in Greater Manchester, a three bed semi in Carlisle and three commercial buildings near Stanstead.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An old Derbyshire farmhouse in Wirksworth gets a stunning make over, a developer builds a pair of semis in Chatham in Kent while a mid-terraced house in Blackhall Colliery in County Durham is given a makeover ready for the rental market.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a stone cottage in Tilton on the Hill in rural Leicestershire while Martin Roberts is at a property auction buy in Croydon on the outskirts of London and Lucy Alexander meets two friends who have bought a bungalow in Rainham in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mid-terrace house in Chaddesdon in Derby, a two-bedroom flat in Londons Maida Vale and a property in Warrington are all sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the new owners to hear what they have planned for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin looks round a pretty cottage with outbuildings with huge potential in Burton upon Trent, while Lucy checks out a flat in Londons Crystal Palace and Dion is in Mossley near Manchester in a four-bedroom bungalow.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin tackles the issue of solar panels on a property in Mountain Ash in Wales and wonders if itll be a sunny outcome for the new owner, while Lucy looks round a barn in Elstree in Hertfordshire and Dion visits a flat in Newport in Shropshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets a couple who buy a former millworkers cottage in Derbyshire as their first home together; Lucy Alexander meets a father and daughter team who are seasoned developers buying a house in Kent; and Dion Dublin talks to a man who, along with two friends, has clubbed together to buy an investment property in Lowestoft in Suffolk.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Walsall in the Midlands looking around a 1950s semi; Martin Roberts meets a man developing a ground-floor one-bedroom flat in Ilford in Essex; and Lucy Alexander catches up on a multi-million-pound redevelopment in East Horsley in Surrey where one small cottage with a big garden is converted into eight separate properties.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Lincolnshire looking round a pair of rural semi-detached cottages which the new owner may convert into one home. programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house in Chesterton in Staffordshire has a smelly problem and needs a total overhaul. programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A husband and wife duo add their design skills to a house in Croydon in Surrey, while a seasoned developer creates a high-end house in multiple occupation in Coventry and a first time developer also does a great job with a house full of period features in Nutall in Nottinghamshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets one of two young brothers busy building a property portfolio in Bolsover, Derbyshire, while Dion Dublin checks out a three-bedroom semi in Wolverhampton, and Lucy Alexander is impressed by plans to convert a two-bedroom, fire-damaged terraced house in Londons Plaistow into a smart five-bedroom property.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.desc: Rob Brydon presents the ultimate quiz battle royale! Ten episodes, 81 contestants - and an epic clash of trivia, tactics, and nerve in the fight to be the last one standing.

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Britains weekday breakfast show on the latest news, weather and sports as well as offering information on entertainment, health and money.

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Lorraine Kelly presents a breakfast programme which includes a topical mix of entertainment, discussion, celebrity interviews, the latest fashion, and more.

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Featuring a generous morning dose of celebrity interviews, showbiz updates, lifestyle tips and everything else relevant to start the day in an upbeat manner.

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: A rotating panel comprised of women from the entertainment and journalism worlds discuss issues from a female perspective, from politics and current affairs to all the latest gossip.

programme.title: ITV Lunchtime News
programme.desc: The latest headlines from around the world. Followed by a national weather forecast and all the news and weather in your region.

programme.title: ITV News London
programme.desc: The latest news in London and the South East, plus local weather.

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Crossing words this time are brother and sister David and Rachel, Merseyside couple Jo and Zak, and Winchester couple Jamie and Arianne.

programme.title: Riddiculous
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh hosts the quiz where contestants take on Riddlemaster Henry Lewis in an attempt to solve a series of riddles - packed full of confusing conundrums and quick-fire general knowledge questions, only one team can make it through to the final and earn the chance to win an exciting cash prize as they take on a series of riddles against the clock. This episode sees Yasmin and Ridwan, Ellie and Katy, and Despoina and Steven attempt to cope with the pressure of the Riddlemasters riddles.

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Contestants play a large coin-drop machine, provided they answer questions correctly. Answer incorrectly and the players could be eliminated. The last contestant standing has an opportunity to win the machines £10,000 jackpot.

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh and his criminally clever Chaser are back for yet another battle of the brains as Albert from Manchester, Phil from Goole, Victoria from Wirral and Liz from Lostwithiel hope that they can beat a Chaser together and leave with thousands of pounds.

programme.title: ITV News London
programme.desc: The latest news in London and the South East, plus local weather.

programme.title: ITV Evening News
programme.desc: The latest headlines from around the world. Followed by a national weather forecast.

programme.title: Mens Six Nations LIVE
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage of the 2026 Guinness Mens Six Nations as Ireland take on Wales at the Aviva Stadium in Dublin - Garry Ringrose was shown a 20-minute red card last year at the Principality Stadium with Ireland 10-3 ahead, and Wales made the most of the visitors being down to 14 men by scoring 15 unanswered points to lead 18-10 thanks to tries from Jac Morgan and Tom Rogers. Six Nations debutant Jamie Osborne then scored a brilliant try for Ireland as they fought back to win 27-18.

programme.title: ITV News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news.

programme.title: ITV News London
programme.desc: The latest news in London and the South East, plus local weather.

programme.title: Bad Boys
programme.desc: Two wisecracking Miami cops are forced to swap identities in order to track down $100 million of heroin stolen from the police. Their only lead is a young woman who can identify the culprits, but when they track her down, she mistakes one of the cops for his partner, forcing the pair into a bizarre and chaotic life swap.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: The Summit
programme.desc: Ben Shephard presents the thrilling reality series where 14 ordinary Brits climb one of New Zealands highest peaks to win a share of 200,000 pounds. The climb and the stakes get ever higher as just seven of the original 14 climbers remain - as game-player Dockers tries to enlist fellow climbers into a secret group to try to oust his biggest threats, trust becomes an issue when some chocolate goes missing and Charlett accuses the others of lying.

programme.title: Bobs Burgers
programme.desc: After a student accuses her of cheating on a test, Tina needs to get back to school to clear her name, but a snowstorm traps her and her family at home.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the worlds longest running primetime drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour. Its themes - love, passion, deception, greed and intrigue - are ones which everyone can relate to.

programme.title: A Touch of Frost

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade travels to one of the few rivers left on the planet that will still test a fisherman - the Congo. He is on the trail of a terrifying legend - the spirit Mami Wata, which is said to lure fishermen from their boats, taking them to the bottom of the river where they are imprisoned for eternity. It sounds far-fetched, but Jeremy soon discovers that the truth is every bit as frightening as the myth.

programme.title: The Invaders
programme.desc: When an alien saucer crashes into a hillside a ranch woman cares for an Invader survivor. Vincent soon hears of the crash and investigates.

programme.title: The Legend of Butch and Sundance
programme.desc: At the turn of the 20th century, a young Butch Cassidy pairs up with the Sundance Kid. They gain a reputation as skilled bandits, but soon start committing crimes that make them the most notorious outlaws ever known.

programme.title: The Arrival
programme.desc: Zane Zaminski is a radio astronomer who believes he has heard an extra-terrestrial signal, but whose discovery is deemed worthless by his boss. Then his research division is closed down due to government cutbacks and he is given the sack. Undeterred, Zane establishes a makeshift radio telescope within his own home and joins forces with fellow scientist Ilana Green to find out more about this alien lifeforce.

programme.title: Star Trek
programme.desc: Captain Kirk is distracted by his passion for a beautiful woman, leaving Spock to defend a planet against a disastrous asteroid collision.

programme.title: The Invaders
programme.desc: Vincent learns that an old army friend is accused of murder and suspects that the supposed victim was an Invader - but that defence wont hold up in court!

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: The team is locked up in Fort Owen to await court martial for the murder of Colonel Morrison. The prosecutor representing the army produces a surprise witness, who claims that Morrison was executed by them for selling classified information.

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A genetic engineer is kidnapped by scientists looking to put her DNA research to dastardly use by creating a race of superwarriors.

programme.title: Kiss of the Dragon
programme.desc: Chinese cop Liu Jian is brought to Paris to help crack a major drugs ring, but when the official with whom hes meant to liaise ends up dead, he realises that corrupt cop Jean-Pierre Richard is the head of the ring. Jian must not only prove he is innocent of the murder but also evade Richards thugs who want him dead. In a city where he knows nobody, Jians only ally is a prostitute named Jessica.

programme.title: War
programme.desc: When ruthless hitman Rogue slaughters an FBI agent and his family, the dead mans partner, Jack Crawford, vows revenge. After three years of hunting shadows, Crawford is no closer to his prey but remains obsessed with the chase, despite having driven away his wife and son. When Rogue finally resurfaces as a gunman for San Franciscos Triads, Crawford leaps at the chance to take down his nemesis.

programme.title: The Power of One
programme.desc: Following the life of English South African boy Peter Philip Kenneth-Keith, in a rural area of the Province of Natal during World War II. Following a series of tragic events, `PK is sent away to boarding school and develops and interest in boxing.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Construction worker William has been trapped under a cherry picker, breaking his leg in three places and crushing his chest. Nick has fallen from a ten-metre ladder onto concrete, landing on his head, resulting in a severe brain injury. Its a matter of life and death and his brother, Nigel, waits nervously at his bedside. Darren has fallen through a window while trying to clean it, cutting his arm badly.

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: At Dalraddy Holiday Park on a traditional Scottish estate at Aviemore, the campers take a train ride, enjoy some quirky Highland games and battle it out on the paintballing field.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The homes of Britain are stacked with unwanted possessions. Henry Cole and Simon OBrien are here to turn them into hard cash. In this episode an old bureau gets a makeover on the road in Yorkshire.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Gill and Paul from Derbyshire dream of owning a holiday home in Benalmádena on Spains Costa del Sol, to escape to during the winter months. Laura Hamilton shows them what their £110k can buy.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Basingstoke. First host, marketing manager and maths enthusiast Nicole, hopes to wow her guests with a culinary journey, from Lebanon to the Caribbean, and a disco for good measure.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Basingstoke, Cheeky business owner Adam brings fun to the fiesta with a menu of authentic Mexican cuisine inspired by his one-week holiday to Cancún, and a water pistol gun fight.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Basingstoke, DJ Mark hosts a festival-themed evening and offers his guests a taste of his own special filtered water. Mark hopes that the good vibes will flow and that the night wont be a washout.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Basingstoke. Winery worker Izzy hosts a night of sophistication, but Mark rejects the black-tie dress code. Can she give her guests a night to remember, or is it too much for some?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Basingstoke, Chris wraps up the week with a 90s music theme and a `Franglais menu. However, with simmering tensions between Mark and Izzy, he hopes that a lip sync battle can win over his guests.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts in Pencader, Wales and the School House bed and breakfast, owned by eco-friendly couple Jayne and Ian, where they hope their environmental approach will win over their guests.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Cathedral 73 in Cardiff, managed by Neil Chidzey and Assistant Manager Jo Goodwin. Guests are wowed by the glamorous rooms, but the costs prove hard to swallow.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For the third visit of the week the group head to Leafy Suburban bed and breakfast in Northwood, where golfer Raj Kumar opens the doors to his home. However, Neil and Jo are not impressed with a surprise visitor.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to The Wellington Inn in Hertfordshire where resources manager Felicity Rainbow takes a spiritual approach to hosting - but the `Welly twist is not a hit with everyone.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its the final day and the BandB owners are meeting to find out what theyve been paid and to settle some scores. After bitter rebukes and apologies, the winner is finally announced.

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: A nine-week-old French bulldog called Kevin competes with a nervous terrier to win the hearts of a loved-up couple. And Rocco the Jack Russell meets an adventurous family who might just fit.

programme.title: The Dog House Australia
programme.desc: Australian shepherd Alfie and the Holmes family are on track to make a new bond, while Missys attachment issues could be too much for a first-time owner.

programme.title: Crufts 2026

programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil look back on some clever builds. In Surrey, Pippas sick of her tatty little bungalow. Can Kirstie help her fall back in love with her home?

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: In Dunstable, John and his wife Helen demolish an old, dilapidated house and in its place, build a million-pound, high-tech, accessible glass pavilion, complete with wildlife garden.

programme.title: The Antiques Riviera
programme.desc: Myles has spent thousands on fake designer furniture; could one be worth millions? A snuff box catches a dealers eye, but will owner Gill be able to part with it?

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Reginald, 78, is dangerously ill. He woke up with severe stomach pain, and the AandE team have concluded that his condition is life-threatening, but can they save him? Other patients include Richard, who has twisted his knee moon-walking in a club, and a young cyclist brought in unconscious after coming off his bike.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Omar, 88, is brought in after suffering severe stomach pains and a suspected abdominal aortic aneurism, also known as a triple A. Meanwhile, Hanny, 33, is rushed in after attempting to take his life with a lethal cocktail of drugs, and during the hectic few hours when the AandE is inundated, staff fight to save an 18-year-old whos been stabbed in a street fight and has lost a life-threatening amount of blood.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: On a busy Saturday, the medical team faces a range of sport and drink-related injuries, but events turn darker when a family party goes horribly wrong, leaving a father and son with life-threatening injuries. Every year 750,000 people are treated at AandE for injuries suffered during recreational activities, mostly at weekends. Ball games account for 300,000 of those, with football the number one culprit.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: Tyreme met the beautiful Tomorrow online over 9 months ago. Tyreme is ready to uproot his life to be with her. Will Nev and Max be able help Tyreme finally meet his Tomorrow?

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: Tee and Ibraheem are so deeply in love that Ibraheem decided to get a neck tattoo of her name. The only problem-Tee lied about her name when they first met! If Ibraheem and his tattoo are real, will their love survive the fallout?

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show
programme.desc: Reese was always successful with women until he met Jesica, a gorgeous girl who was doing more than playing hard to get. When a shady set of phone numbers sparks Reeses suspicions, he calls on Nev and Kamie to investigate.

programme.desc: After being paralysed as a teenager, Robert met Ashleigh online and they fell in love. With news he may not have much longer to live, Robert asks Nev and Max to bring Ashleigh to him.

programme.title: Peppa Pig

programme.title: British Gangsters: Faces of the Underworld
programme.desc: When innocent people are jailed for crimes they did not commit, what effect does it have on their lives and their loved ones? Bernard OMahoney finds out.

programme.title: Heartland Homicide
programme.desc: Fredrick Banks is an eccentric senior who loves to show off his vintage cars and wallet stuffed with cash. When he is found slain in his garage, authorities hunt for the mysterious stranger who has recently been spotted lurking in town.

programme.title: Heartland Homicide
programme.desc: A fire engulfs the home of the Klaus family in the middle the night while everyone is asleep, and the investigators suspect that it isnt an accident. The whole case changes when the son starts seeing the spirits of his dead family.

programme.title: Heartland Homicide
programme.desc: When Abbie Speirs body is pulled from her burning house in a quaint farming community, investigators quickly discover that the fire is no accident, and that a killer could be lurking closer to home than anyone can imagine.

programme.title: True Crime Scene
programme.desc: A mother of two is charged after placing a 911 call stating her two sons have been stabbed by an intruder.

programme.title: True Crime Scene
programme.desc: Woman found dead and her husband who was drunk that evening confesses to her murder but none of the evidence points to him.

programme.title: True Crime Scene
programme.desc: Ted Binion at the Las Vegas casino tells his lawyer to remove his girlfriends Sandys name from his the will if she doesnt kill him tonight.

programme.title: The Feared: Irish Gangsters
programme.desc: True crime author Bernard OMahoney returns to his home country to shine a light on the Irish underworld. With exclusive access to high-profile Irish `faces, he enters uncharted territory when he discovers that there may be more to these crimes than meets the eye. He travels around the country through the workings of a dark criminal underworld with stories of extreme violence, poverty, and ultimately, the devastating consequences they bring.

programme.title: British Gangsters: Faces of the Underworld
programme.desc: Bernard looks at the effect of time on gangs and their origins. Whether it be motorcycle gangs or Irish travellers, the roots are dying out with every new generation.

programme.desc: In Norfolk, an otherwise unassuming summer evening is shaken by an armed robbery. A vehicle is stolen and innocent people are shot.

programme.title: The Trackers Diary: Bears of Katmai
programme.desc: Every fall in the northern Pacific, up to a million salmon migrate back to the rivers and streams where they were born to spawn. Many of these streams are located in Katmai National Park, Alaska.

programme.title: The Trackers Diary: Bears of Katmai
programme.desc: Everyone hates biting insects, but if people were to look a little closer, they would be able to see that even the tiniest and most irritating bug can be essential for a working ecosystem.

programme.title: The Trackers Diary: Bears of Katmai
programme.desc: Casey and the crew go out in search of the family of the lost cub from the previous night. They hope that if they are able to find the cubs family, the cub will have been able to find them as well.

programme.title: Facing Grizzlies
programme.desc: Filmmaker Phil Timpanys redemption story follows him as he turns from being a big-game bear hunter into a passionate advocate for grizzlies. The story of the heartfelt filmmaking that went into documenting Sophie and her cubs.

programme.title: In the Land of the Moose
programme.desc: At the end of the summer, a moose finds herself alone in the forest. She has just lost her calf, probably to a bear. Now she must find a mate. Once pregnant again, she will endure months of bone-chilling winter until she delivers in the spring.

programme.title: The Secret Lives of Big Cats
programme.desc: An examination of pumas, which are distantly related to lions and much more closely related to cheetahs.

programme.title: The Secret Lives of Big Cats
programme.desc: An examination of lions, which are highly social and form prides of up to 35 animals, unlike other cats.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters

programme.desc: Ginny Buckley is house hunting in Herefordshire with a couple who have a budget of £330k and want to move from the coast to the countryside. Ginny also finds out how a mobile cider press is helping locals to turn their own windfall fruit into a tasty tipple.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew travels north on a road trip for an epic private house clearance in a remote part of Scotland. He also visits a historic cutlery factory in Sheffield.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew haggles with a windmill manager in Kent, visits a hip dealer in Manchester and seeks artefact redemption at a Somerset abbey. He also moves shop after ten years in the same place.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew visits Scotland to see a Victorian theatre in Aberdeen and the remote town of Rothes. Also, sidekick Tee shows him how to do a deal at a steam railway in Devon.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Paul Laidlaw continue their road trip in Northern Ireland.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts take a road trip around the UK with the aim of finding treasures to sell.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Art conservator Lucia Scalisi revives an oil painting of a mining scene, created by an amateur artist in the 1960s. It is a delicate task for Lucia as she cleans away the dirty surface to reveal the stunning scene underneath. Owner Gwyn is overwhelmed when she returns to the barn to see her miner fathers artwork restored to its full glory.

programme.title: Celebrity Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Auctioneer Angus Ashworth and his team help celebrities clear their homes of clutter. Will they discover hidden gems to be auctioned off for serious profit?

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Two stories shaped by moments from British history: a family torn apart by a wartime baby and a daughter given up against a backdrop of racism. Barbara always believed that she was one of three children and in her 20s she made a startling discovery.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: This time, the house hunters are Veronica and Jude from north London. Veronicas parents come from Galicia in northern Spain, but this couple want to find somewhere warmer, so theyre heading south in search of their perfect holiday home.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew heads to Devon, where he finds a wealth of unusual oddities housed in a country pub. Next, its on to Hastings, which has enjoyed a recent resurgence in the antiques trade.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Seeing an untapped source of rare items, Drew tours Catalonia looking for some chic Spanish antiques. In Peralada, Drew is blown away by a 17th Century house full of treasures.

programme.title: Help! My House is Haunted
programme.desc: Emma is harassed by spirits. One sinister spirit is Shadow Man, and he wont give her up without a fight, so Ian takes extreme measures to save her.

programme.title: Help! My House is Haunted

programme.title: Help! My House is Haunted
programme.desc: Tim, Christine and their three children have lived in their Bristol house for 25 years. But something sinister has been increasingly taunting them. Desperate for help, they call in the team, who uncover that something inhuman could be at play.

programme.title: Help! My House is Haunted

programme.desc: At the home of the Edinburgh International Festival. Violinist Nicola Benedetti introduces a jazz special headlined by Grammy-winning musician Wynton Marsalis.

programme.title: Up Late With Nicola Benedetti
programme.desc: At the home of the Edinburgh International Festival. Violinist Nicola Benedetti introduces a jazz special headlined by Grammy-winning musician Wynton Marsalis.

programme.title: The Fall Guy
programme.desc: Battle-scarred stuntman Colt Seavers springs back into action when a movie star suddenly disappears. As the mystery deepens, he soon finds himself ensnared in a sinister plot that pushes him to the edge of a fall more dangerous than any stunt.

programme.title: Rampage
programme.desc: Primatologist Davis Okoye shares an unshakable bond with George, an extraordinarily intelligent silverback gorilla thats been in his care since birth. When a genetic experiment goes wrong, it causes George, a wolf and a reptile to grow to a monstrous size. As the mutated beasts embark on a path of destruction, Okoye teams up with a discredited genetic engineer and the military to secure an antidote and prevent a global catastrophe.

programme.title: The Fast and the Furious: Tokyo Drift
programme.desc: Street-racing sequel following an adrenaline-junkie automobile addict who narrowly avoids jail by agreeing to move in with his father in Tokyo. However, he soon discovers the thrill of the local underground drift racing scene and unwittingly becomes involved with the Yakuza.

programme.title: Fast and Furious
programme.desc: Dominic Toretto is forced to come out of hiding when a loved one is brutally murdered. Heading to LA to track down the culprit, he crosses paths with Brian OConnor, the FBI agent whose undercover infiltration of Torettos gang led to the motorised miscreants exile across the border. The two men temporarily put their differences aside as they investigate the killing.

programme.title: 300: Rise of an Empire
programme.desc: Greek Gen. Themistocles leads the charge to fight invading Persian forces led by the god Xerxes -- once a mortal -- and Artemisia.

programme.title: xXx: The Return of Xander Cage
programme.desc: After coming out of self-imposed exile, daredevil operative Xander Cage must race against time to recover a sinister weapon known as Pandoras Box, a device that controls every military satellite in the world. Recruiting a new group of thrill-seeking cohorts, Xander finds himself entangled in a deadly conspiracy that points to collusion at the highest levels of government.

programme.title: Fast and Furious 6
programme.desc: Since Dom and Brians heist in Rio they must live as fugitives. Meanwhile, Agent Hobbs has been tracking a gang of lethally skilled mercenary drivers. Hobbs asks Dom and his crew for help in exchange for full pardons for everyone.

programme.title: Rampage
programme.desc: Primatologist Davis Okoye shares an unshakable bond with George, an extraordinarily intelligent silverback gorilla thats been in his care since birth. When a genetic experiment goes wrong, it causes George, a wolf and a reptile to grow to a monstrous size. As the mutated beasts embark on a path of destruction, Okoye teams up with a discredited genetic engineer and the military to secure an antidote and prevent a global catastrophe.

programme.title: Godzilla x Kong: The New Empire
programme.desc: Godzilla and the almighty Kong face a colossal threat hidden deep within the planet, challenging their very existence and the survival of the human race.

programme.title: Spider-Man
programme.desc: Adaptation of the adventures of the swinging comic-strip hero. A shy student becomes a crime fighter after a bite from a mutant spider, during a school outing, gives him extraordinary powers. As he comes to terms with his new-found abilities, he is also forced to contend with a maniac villain known as the Green Goblin.

programme.title: Fast and Furious Presents: Hobbs and Shaw
programme.desc: Brixton Lorr is a cybernetically enhanced soldier who possesses superhuman strength, a brilliant mind and a lethal pathogen that could wipe out half of the worlds population. Its now up to hulking lawman Luke Hobbs and lawless operative Deckard Shaw to put aside their past differences and work together to prevent the seemingly indestructible Lorr from destroying humanity.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade
programme.desc: The intrepid explorer Indiana Jones sets out to rescue his father, a medievalist who has vanished while searching for the Holy Grail. Following clues in the old mans notebook, Indy arrives in Venice, where he enlists the help of a beautiful academic, but they are not the only ones who are on the trail, and some sinister old enemies soon come out of the woodwork.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Crystal Skull
programme.desc: Professor Henry Jones is given a coded message during the Cold War which leads him to Peru in search of a legendary skull. Jones needs to deliver the skull to its rightful place, but Soviet agents who are trailing Jones have other plans which will endanger the West if the artefact falls into their hands.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Crystal Skull

Jones needs to deliver the skull to its rightful place, but Soviet agents who are trailing Jones have other plans which will endanger the West if the artefact falls into their hands.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go! and DC Super Hero Girls: Mayhem in the Multiverse
programme.desc: The Super Hero Girls and the Teen Titans reluctantly band together to fight a new threat to the universe.

programme.title: Buffalo Kids
programme.desc: Arriving in New York City in 1886, two orphaned Irish siblings embark on a wild cross-country journey aboard a transcontinental train. They make a new friend and encounter heroes and villains in their quest to find a new home.

programme.title: Straight Outta Nowhere: Scooby-Doo Meets Courage the Cowardly Dog
programme.desc: Scooby-Doo and the Gang find themselves in the middle of Nowhere, Kansas, the backwoods hometown of Courage and his owners, Muriel and Eustace Bagge.

programme.title: Fox and Hare Save the Forest
programme.desc: One day, Fox and Hare discover that Owl has disappeared. They go looking for him in the forest with all their friends and discover a big lake that wasnt there before. It is the beginning of an exciting and funny adventure.

programme.title: Monsters vs Aliens
programme.desc: When an evil alien attacks the Earth, a motley crew of monsters is called into action to save the world from imminent destruction. A DreamWorks animation.

programme.title: Monster Family 2
programme.desc: Baba Yaga and Renfield are captured by a monster hunter so the Wishbone family once again transform into a vampire Frankensteins monster a mummy and a werewolf in a bid to free them.

programme.title: Despicable Me 4
programme.desc: Gru welcomes a new member to the family, Gru Jr., whos intent on tormenting his dad. However, their peaceful existence soon comes crashing down when criminal mastermind Maxime Le Mal escapes from prison and vows revenge against Gru.

programme.title: Turbo
programme.desc: A snail gets the chance to escape his slow-paced life and race in the Indianapolis 500 when a freak accident gives him the power of superspeed.

programme.title: The LEGO Movie 2: The Second Part
programme.desc: The citizens of Bricksburg face a dangerous new threat when LEGO DUPLO invaders from outer space start to wreck everything in their path. The battle to defeat the enemy and restore harmony to the LEGO universe takes Emmet, Lucy, Batman and the rest of their friends to faraway, unexplored worlds that test their courage and creativity.

programme.title: Rango
programme.desc: An ordinary chameleon, Rango, winds up in the town of Dirt, a lawless outpost in the Wild West in need of a new sheriff.

programme.title: Miss Congeniality 2: Armed and Fabulous
programme.desc: FBI agent Gracie is given another makeover to become the face of the Bureau, prior to embarking on a promotional tour. When two of her friends are kidnapped - Miss USA and a beauty pageant director - she decides to investigate on her own, in defiance of orders to the contrary.

programme.title: Hope Springs
programme.desc: A woman drags her skeptical husband to a renowned counsellors marriage retreat to try to put the spark back in their relationship.

programme.title: Ferris Buellers Day Off
programme.desc: A teen perpetrates a series of elaborate deceptions to ensure that he and two friends spend a carefree day in Chicago.

programme.title: The Intern
programme.desc: Ben Whittaker, a retired widower in his 70s becomes senior intern at a fashion based e-commerce company. Jules Ostin, the founder, while first hesitant, soon learns to respect and even like her new intern despite their age difference.

programme.title: Mean Machine
programme.desc: A footballer is jailed for throwing a crucial game. Whilst inside, the football-loving warden sets him the task of coaching a team of prisoners to take on the all-conquering warders side. Can he form a disparate bunch of prisoners into a winning team?

programme.title: White Chicks
programme.desc: Disgraced FBI agents Marcus and Kevin go to bizarre lengths to catch a devious millionaire. They disguise themselves as dumb white socialites Brittany and Tiffany Wilson, the heiresses to a hotel fortune who are under threat. Gross-out comedic farce from the makers of `Scary Movie.

programme.title: The Man With Two Brains
programme.desc: Zany horror spoof about a brilliant brain surgeon who falls in love with a female brain stored in a jar. The brain is part of an impressive collection held by a colleague in Austria and, upon learning of his wifes infidelity, the surgeon allows himself to be charmed. Communicating telepathically with one of the specimens in the laboratory, he falls in love - but needs a body to put it in.

programme.title: The Wedding Singer
programme.desc: When one-time rock hopeful Robbie is jilted at the altar, he vents his bitterness in song, even when singing at other peoples weddings. Only true love can redeem the broken hearted songster but fortunately, hes about to meet a woman who will spin him right round (like a record).

programme.title: Ted 2
programme.desc: Teddy bear Ted seeks legal help from a young lawyer and a legendary, civil-rights attorney when the law declares him to be property and not a person.

programme.title: Bridget Joness Baby
programme.desc: Befuddled mom-to-be Bridget Jones must figure out which of the two men in her life is the father of her child.

programme.title: American Pie: Reunion
programme.desc: Outrageous antics ensue when Jim, Michelle, Stifler and the gang get back together for their 13-year high school reunion.

programme.title: Jackass Presents: Bad Grandpa
programme.desc: Irving Zisman takes his young and impressionable grandson on a cross-country trip in which they encounter male strippers, biker-bar patrons and other unsuspecting citizens.

programme.title: Jackass: The Movie
programme.desc: Johnny Knoxville and his mad companions leap onto the big screen. Expect the usual mad gross-out japes, from making snow cones out of urine to using the display toilets in bathroom outlets, and from racing golf carts over hallowed greens to tightrope walking over alligators.

programme.title: Jackass: The Movie
programme.desc: Johnny Knoxville and his mad companions leap onto the big screen. Expect the usual mad gross-out japes, from making snow cones out of urine to using the display toilets in bathroom outlets, and from racing golf carts over hallowed greens to tightrope walking over alligators.

programme.title: The Damned United
programme.desc: The story of Brian Cloughs 44 day stint as manager of Leeds United Football Club. England, in 1974. When Don Revie quits Leeds to become the England boss, the outspoken Brian Clough takes charge. Determined to impose his own style upon Revies tough tackling team, Clough soon alienates his players and the board. Based on the book by David Pearce.

programme.title: A Star Is Born
programme.desc: Hard-drinking country music star Jackson Maine discovers, and falls in love with, a struggling but talented singer named Ally. As her career quickly takes off, Jackson starts to realize that his best days may be behind him.

programme.title: Burning an Illusion
programme.desc: A young British-born black woman begins to question her attitude to love and life, and her desire for middle-class respectability and security through marriage.

programme.title: An Officer and a Gentleman
programme.desc: At a tough training school for American navy pilots, one of the new intake falls foul of the hard-as-nails drill sergeant, but takes it on the chin, and finds the additional strength to help a fellow student who is feeling the pressure.

programme.title: Empire of the Sun
programme.desc: Young Jim has to grow up fast after the Japanese invade Shanghai. Separated from his parents and alone in a prisoner of war camp, Jim must fight to survive.

When Don Revie quits Leeds to become the England boss, the outspoken Brian Clough takes charge. Determined to impose his own style upon Revies tough tackling team, Clough soon alienates his players and the board. Based on the book by David Pearce.

programme.title: It Ends With Us
programme.desc: Lily Bloom falls for a charming neurosurgeon after she moves to Boston to chase her dream of opening her own business. However, when her first love suddenly reenters her life, her relationship gets upended, leaving her with an impossible choice.

programme.title: A Star Is Born
programme.desc: Hard-drinking country music star Jackson Maine discovers, and falls in love with, a struggling but talented singer named Ally. As her career quickly takes off, Jackson starts to realize that his best days may be behind him.

programme.title: Nuremberg
programme.desc: As the Nuremberg trials are set to begin, a U.S. Army psychiatrist gets locked in a dramatic psychological showdown with accused Nazi war criminal Hermann Goring.

programme.title: I, Tonya
programme.desc: In 1991, talented figure skater Tonya Harding becomes the first American woman to complete a triple axel during a competition. In 1994, her world comes crashing down when her ex-husband conspires to injure Nancy Kerrigan, a fellow Olympic hopeful, in a poorly conceived attack that forces the young woman to withdraw from the national championship. Hardings life and legacy instantly become tarnished as shes forever associated with one of the most infamous scandals in sports history.

programme.title: American Sniper
programme.desc: Based on the autobiography by Chris Kyle.

programme.title: Burning an Illusion
programme.desc: A young British-born black woman begins to question her attitude to love and life, and her desire for middle-class respectability and security through marriage.

programme.title: Burning an Illusion
